title: Keep messages scoped to the current checkout/worktree in V1
context: Phase 6 adds Git/worktree awareness. A repository can have multiple worktrees, and each worktree has its own checkout copy of .waystation. The project needed to decide whether V1 introduces shared cross-worktree message storage or keeps messages checkout-local.
decision: V1 keeps messages, claims, handoffs, events, and other ledger records scoped to the current checkout/worktree. Separate worktrees see each other's message records through Git merges, not through a live shared inbox.
consequences: No shared message store, daemon, or cross-worktree locking is introduced in Phase 6.
Git/worktree integration should make the current checkout/worktree visible in CLI, MCP, dashboard, and briefs.
Users working in separate worktrees must merge or inspect the other worktree to see its latest ledger records.
A shared cross-worktree inbox can be reconsidered later if real workflow pressure justifies the added complexity.
